title: What Scripture actually says about leading
caption:
You were handed a distorted picture of leadership.

The version you inherited looked like authority from the top — make the calls, hold the title, have people under you. That's not the model Scripture gives. The model Scripture gives is a man who walks to the cost first.

First into the apology. First to absorb the hard thing. First to show up when it's inconvenient. First to serve the people he leads rather than manage them from a safe distance.

Christ didn't command the cross. He walked to it. That's leadership — not performance of authority, but absorption of cost on behalf of the people you're responsible for.

"Whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant." — Matthew 20:26

If you're going to lead anyone — a family, a team, a friendship — lead the way this shows.

#faith #christianmen #godlymen #manofgod #leadership #servantleadership #scripture #christianity #masculinity #disciplemaking
theme: He leads by going first into the cost.
